spec_match.rationale: The server validator connects to a real backend endpoint to verify the WebCrypto JWT signature and run all six essential claims checks.

interactive.rationale: Users can load dynamically signed sample tokens and toggle expected fields (nonce, audience) to inspect PASS/FAIL checklist behavior in real-time.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Aligns perfectly with the need for Relying Parties to understand and implement strict cryptographic and claims validation.

edge_cases.rationale: Explicitly lets developers explore critical validation failure modes (nonce mismatches, invalid audience, signature corruption) on the live checklist.

references.rationale: The concept page does not cite the required chromestatus.com/feature/5205725253074944 reference link.

design_system.rationale: Highly conformant styling, utilizing clean layouts, AA contrast states, and standard forms and banners.

openQuestions.title: Add missing chromestatus.com reference link
openQuestions.detail: Every concept page must include a link to the associated ChromeStatus entry. Add a link to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5205725253074944 in the references list or crumbs block.

summary: The server-validator is a gold-standard interactive developer utility that demonstrates cryptographic JWT checkups. Its only compliance issue is the missing ChromeStatus link.
